tools.name: search_docs_rag
tools.description: Search OPC UA specification documentation and get an AI-synthesized answer grounded by the knowledge base. Uses Azure AI Search retrieval + GPT-4o for natural language answers with specification references. Best for general questions about OPC UA concepts, protocol details, services, security models, or any specification content. For structured NodeSet queries (specific ObjectTypes, Variables, Methods), use search_nodes instead.

query.description: Natural language question about OPC UA specifications

context.description: Optional prior conversation context for follow-up questions, alternating user/assistant messages separated by newlines

tools.name: search_nodes
tools.description: Search OPC UA NodeSet nodes with structured filters. Use this to find specific ObjectTypes, Variables, Methods, or DataTypes by name, spec, parent type, or modelling rule. Version-aware with two-pass fallback to older versions.

query.description: Text query to search node names and descriptions

node_class.description: Filter by node class: ObjectType, Variable, Method, DataType, Object, VariableType, ReferenceType

spec.description: Filter by companion spec name (e.g., DI, Pumps, PlasticsRubber)

parent_type.description: Filter by parent type browse name

modelling_rule.description: Filter by modelling rule: Mandatory, Optional, MandatoryPlaceholder, OptionalPlaceholder

source.description: Filter by source: 'opcfoundation' (official specs) or 'cloudlib' (UA-CloudLibrary submissions)

spec_version.description: Filter by specific spec version (e.g., v104, v105). Overrides version_mode.

tools.name: search_docs
tools.description: Search OPC UA specification documentation (HTML pages, tables, diagrams). Use this for questions about OPC UA concepts, protocol details, services, or any specification text content. Version-aware.

query.description: Search query about OPC UA specifications

spec.description: Optional spec part filter (e.g., Part4, Part5, DI, PackML)

spec_version.description: Filter by specific spec version (e.g., v104, v105). Overrides version_mode.

tools.name: get_type_hierarchy
tools.description: Get the type hierarchy for an OPC UA ObjectType including supertype chain, declared members, inherited members, and total member counts. Use this to answer questions like 'what is the largest ObjectType' or 'show the inheritance chain for ServerType'.

type_name.description: ObjectType browse name to look up (e.g., DesignType, ServerType, DeviceType)

spec.description: Optional companion spec to narrow results (e.g., DI, Pumps)

tools.name: get_spec_summary
tools.description: Get NodeSet statistics for a specific OPC UA companion specification or all specs combined. Returns ObjectType/Variable/Method/DataType counts and top ObjectTypes by total members (declared + inherited).

spec.description: Companion spec name (e.g., DI, Pumps, PlasticsRubber). Omit for cross-spec master summary.

tools.name: count_nodes
tools.description: Count and aggregate OPC UA NodeSet nodes by facets. Returns counts grouped by node class, companion spec, modelling rule, data type, or source.

facet.description: Facet to group by: node_class, spec_part, modelling_rule, data_type, source

node_class.description: Optional filter by node class: ObjectType, Variable, Method, DataType

spec.description: Optional filter by companion spec name

spec_version.description: Filter by specific spec version (e.g., v104, v105). Overrides version_mode.

tools.name: list_specs
tools.description: List all indexed OPC UA companion specifications and CloudLibrary NodeSets. Returns a ranked catalog with name, source (opcfoundation vs cloudlib), version, node count, popularity (downloads), and description. When listing CloudLib, cross-queries opcfoundation to annotate each entry with version comparison.

source.description: Filter by source: 'opcfoundation' (official specs) or 'cloudlib' (UA-CloudLibrary). Omit for all.

unique_to_source.description: When true AND source='cloudlib', only return cloudlib entries whose namespace is NOT in opcfoundation OR with different version

version_mode.description: Version mode: 'latest' (default), 'all' to include all versions

tools.name: validate_nodeset
tools.description: Validate an OPC UA NodeSet XML against the OPC UA standard and OPC 11030 Modelling Best Practices. Checks naming conventions, modelling rules, type hierarchy, reference types, and namespace conventions.

nodeset_xml.description: The NodeSet XML content to validate

tools.name: compare_versions
tools.description: Compare two versions of an OPC UA companion specification's NodeSet to identify added, removed, and changed nodes. Classifies changes as backward-compatible or breaking per OPC 11030 §3 (Rules for backward compatibility). Use this to assess migration impact between spec versions.

spec.description: Companion spec name (e.g., DI, Pumps, PlasticsRubber)

node_class.description: Node class to compare (optional, e.g., ObjectType, Variable). Default: all.

tools.name: check_compliance
tools.description: Check whether a NodeSet XML implementation is compliant with a companion specification's type definitions. Compares the implementation against the indexed spec to find missing mandatory nodes, missing optional nodes, and extra nodes. Use this to verify that an OPC UA server correctly implements a companion specification.

nodeset_xml.description: The NodeSet XML content of the implementation to check

spec.description: Companion spec name to check against (e.g., DI, Pumps, PlasticsRubber, Machinery)

object_type.description: Specific ObjectType to check compliance for (optional — checks all types if omitted)

tools.name: suggest_model
tools.description: Suggest an OPC UA information model design based on a description of the domain or device to model. Recommends ObjectTypes, Variables, Methods, DataTypes, and which existing companion specs and base types to reuse (DI, Machinery, IA, etc.). Follows OPC 11030 Modelling Best Practices. Use this when starting a new companion specification or extending an existing one.

description.description: Description of the domain, device, or system to model (e.g., 'a CNC milling machine with spindle speed, tool changer, and job management')

focus.description: Specific aspect to focus on (optional, e.g., 'state machine', 'alarms', 'data types')
